title: "RAID0 Strange Behavior"
date: 2009-06-05
forum: Hardware

### Post by mooreted on 2009-06-05
This is the first time I've set up RAID so maybe there's just a lot I don't know. I have been reading as much as I can, but there is a lot of conflicting information out there.

I have a Gateway GT5662, AMD Phenom 9500+ Quad-Core, Nvidia 8800GT, two 160GB SATA drives in RAID0.

I set up the RAID in my BIOS. Used the alternate Ubuntu CD which asked me if I wanted to use the RAID configuration, I said Yes and continued with the install.

Everything is running fine except:

Copying a 6GB file showed as copying at 30Mbps. Shouldn't it be faster?

fdisk -l shows "Unable to seek on /dev/sda"

System monitor shows the hard drive as /dev/mapper/nvidia_dccdffje1 (strange)

hdparm -Tt /dev/sda

/dev/sda:
 Timing cached reads:   4076 MB in  2.00 seconds = 2038.42 MB/sec
 Timing buffered disk reads:  318 MB in  3.01 seconds = 105.58 MB/sec

dmraid -n

Is everything working like it should, or is something wrong?

### Post by mooreted on 2009-06-06
Okay, hdparm says:

/dev/sda:
 Timing cached reads:   3954 MB in  2.00 seconds = 1977.74 MB/sec
 Timing buffered disk reads:  318 MB in  3.01 seconds = 105.58 MB/sec

I will just assume the raid is working as it should. 

Subjectively, some things seem much quicker and others about the same. I do load my music library faster, which is nice. Thumbnails in directories with lots of pics load faster and, it seems, in big chunks instead of one at a time.

I'd say my raid experiment was a success. Fortunately I have backups of everything just in case.

I still don't completely understand the output of some of these more advanced benchmark tools.

---
